sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Terminology 19:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Terminology 19:1
items.html: When the lights of ZAT also operate in the Rosh, which is called GAR since the lights of GAR that should be in the Rosh are absent there, it is called “lowering of the Rosh [head],” meaning that the Rosh is lowered to the same level as the ZAT, which are called Guf [body].
